items.headline: Nine new homes proposed for Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: The City Council heard a proposal to subdivide a 1-acre vacant lot into nine single-family homes and seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This is a significant residential development that will add 16 new dwelling units to the area.

items.headline: City approves lease revenue bonds for new Public Safety Building
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved financing for a new Public Safety Building project on April 28. This is a major capital improvement that will affect public safety infrastructure in the city.

items.headline: Council to review Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan
items.summary: City Council held a study session on April 28 to review a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan. This plan will shape how Mountain View manages its trees, green spaces, and wildlife habitat going forward.

items.headline: Zingsters restaurant opens at 220 University Ave
items.summary: A new restaurant called Zingsters is opening in downtown Palo Alto's commercial district. This represents new dining options in the heart of downtown.

items.headline: Sunnyvale plans 67-unit mixed-use development with retail
items.summary: A 4.22-acre site in Sunnyvale would be developed with 55 townhomes, 12 apartment flats, and 18,535 square feet of retail space including a new two-story building, while preserving the existing Speedy's Tacos. The project requires subdivision into 17 lots and was presented to the Planning Commission as a study session.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered revising business license tax rates in the municipal code as part of the annual fiscal update. The outcome of this vote will affect how much local businesses pay in licensing fees to operate in Campbell.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves temporary supergraphic signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: The city authorized temporary large-scale signage on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during World Cup 2026 events. This is a limited-time exception to normal sign regulations to accommodate the international sporting event.

items.headline: Santa Clara approves new firefighters contract through 2029
items.summary: The city council approved a three-year labor agreement with the Santa Clara Firefighters union (Local 1171) covering 2026-2029. The contract terms were not detailed in this agenda item.
